title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide introduces Gen Z as 'Digital Natives' who experience life through an online lens. It presents statistics on daily social media platform usage among students and frames constant connectivity as an indispensable modern reality.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
70% of students engage with Instagram and Snapchat
Constant connectivity is essentially indispensable in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart correlating heavy social media usage with negative mental health outcomes, citing data from a 2025 Mental Health Statistics Report.
key_claims: 87% of users have usage exceeding 2 hours per day
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying, each described in a separate card with an icon.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant exposure to curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital connection: digital detox, user awareness, and platform responsibility.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
